revision.text:
'''タケヌナカワワケ'''は『[[日本書紀]]』では'''武渟川別'''、『[[古事記]]』では'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）と表記される、記紀に登場する人物。[[四道将軍]]の一人。[[大彦命]]の子。阿倍臣の祖。
[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった大毘古命と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが会津の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。

'''タケヌナカワワケ'''、'''タケヌナカワワケノミコト'''は『[[日本書紀]]』では'''武渟川別'''、『[[古事記]]』では'''建沼河別命'''と表記される、記紀に登場する人物。'''武渟川別命'''。[[四道将軍]]の一人。[[大彦命]]の子。阿倍臣の祖。
[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった大毘古命と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが会津の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。

[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった大毘古命と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが会津の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。
[[Category:日本神話の人物|たけぬなかわわけ]]

[[Category:日本神話の人物|たけぬなかわわけ]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|たけぬなかわわけ]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

'''タケヌナカワワケ'''、'''タケヌナカワワケノミコト'''は『[[日本書紀]]』では'''武渟川別'''、『[[古事記]]』では'''建沼河別命'''と表記される、記紀に登場する人物。'''武渟川別命'''。崇神朝の[[四道将軍]]の一人で、垂仁朝には和珥臣の祖・彦国葺、中臣連の祖・大鹿島、物部連の祖・十千根、大伴連の祖・武日と並び大夫の一人だった。[[大彦命]]の子。[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]の祖。
[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった[[大彦命|大毘古命]]と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが[[会津]]の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。
[[Category:日本神話の人物|たけぬなかわわけ]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|たけぬなかわわけ]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

'''タケヌナカワワケ'''、'''タケヌナカワワケノミコト'''は『[[日本書紀]]』では'''武渟川別'''、『[[古事記]]』では'''建沼河別命'''と表記される、記紀に登場する人物。'''武渟川別命'''。崇神朝の[[四道将軍]]の一人で、垂仁朝には和珥臣の祖・彦国葺、中臣連の祖・大鹿島、物部連の祖・十千根、大伴連の祖・武日と並び大夫の一人だった。[[大彦命]]の子。[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]の祖。
[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった[[大彦命|大毘古命]]と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが[[会津]]の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。
{{デフォルトソート:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

'''タケヌナカワワケ'''、'''タケヌナカワワケノミコト'''は、古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。『[[日本書紀]]』では'''武渟川別'''、『[[古事記]]』では'''建沼河別命'''と表記される。'''武渟川別命'''とも。[[崇神天皇|崇神]]朝の[[四道将軍]]の一人で、[[垂仁天皇|垂仁]]朝には和珥臣の祖・彦国葺、中臣連の祖・大鹿島、物部連の祖・十千根、大伴連の祖・武日と並び大夫の一人。[[大彦命]]の子。[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]の祖。
[[崇神天皇]]のとき、将軍として東海に派遣される。『古事記』によると、高志（越）の国の平定に向かった大毘古命（大彦命）と相津（会津）で出会ったとされ、これが[[会津]]の地名の由来という。
また、天皇の命により[[吉備津彦]]と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅した。
{{デフォルトソート:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 武渟川別 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の[[王子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = {{JPN}} | 死亡日 = 不詳 | 没地 = {{JPN}} | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = | 配偶者1 = 素性不詳 | 配偶者2 = | 配偶者3 = | 配偶者4 = | 配偶者5 = | 配偶者6 = | 配偶者7 = | 配偶者8 = | 配偶者9 = | 配偶者10 = | 子女 = | 父親 = [[大彦命]]（第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]） | 母親 = 素性不詳 | 役職 = [[四道将軍]] | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''武渟川別'''（たけぬなかわわけ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「武渟川別」「武渟河別」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）」と表記される。 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の子で、[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣（阿倍氏）]]の祖。[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[東海道|東海]]に派遣されたほか、[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられる。 __TOC__{{-}} == 記録 ==
'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}}[[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}}[[孝元天皇]]|02=[[吉備津彦命]]
［西道］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}}[[開化天皇]]|02=[[大彦命]]
［北陸］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[彦坐王|○]]|02={{Supra|10}}[[崇神天皇]]|03=[[御間城姫|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}]]|04='''武渟川別'''
［東海］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01=[[丹波道主命]]
（丹波）|02={{Supra|11}}[[垂仁天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・大鹿島（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の大臣命」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[宝賀寿男]]|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:阿倍氏]]

『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・大鹿島（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の大臣命」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[宝賀寿男]]|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:阿倍氏]]

'''武渟川別'''（たけぬなかわわけ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「武渟川別」「武渟河別」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）」と表記される。 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の子で、[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣（阿倍氏）]]の祖。[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[東海道|東海]]に派遣されたほか、[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられる。 __TOC__{{-}} == 記録 ==

revision.text:
'''武渟川別'''（たけぬなかわわけ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「武渟川別」「武渟河別」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）」と表記される。 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の子で、[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣（阿倍氏）]]の祖。[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[東海道|東海]]に派遣されたほか、[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられる。 __TOC__{{-}} == 記録 ==
'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[孝元天皇]]|02=[[吉備津彦命]]
［西道］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[開化天皇]]|02=[[大彦命]]
［北陸］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[彦坐王|○]]|02={{Supra|10}} [[崇神天皇]]|03=[[御間城姫|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}]]|04='''武渟川別'''
［東海］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01=[[丹波道主命]]
（丹波）|02={{Supra|11}} [[垂仁天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・大鹿島（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の大臣命」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[宝賀寿男]]|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:阿倍氏]]

『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の大臣命」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[宝賀寿男]]|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:阿倍氏]]

[[File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より武渟川別の想像図]] '''武渟川別'''（たけぬなかわわけ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「武渟川別」「武渟河別」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）」と表記される。 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の子で、[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣（阿倍氏）]]の祖。[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[東海道|東海]]に派遣されたほか、[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられる。 __TOC__{{-}} == 記録 ==

『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の大臣命」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[宝賀寿男]]|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:阿倍氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

［北陸］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[彦坐王|○]]|02={{Supra|10}} [[崇神天皇]]|03=[[御間城姫|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}]]|04='''武渟川別'''
［東海］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01=[[丹波道主命]]
（丹波）|02={{Supra|11}} [[垂仁天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}

『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の[[大臣命]]」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:阿倍氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

（丹波）|02={{Supra|11}} [[垂仁天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 [[武蔵御嶽神社]]は崇神天皇7年に武渟川別が東方十二道平定の折、[[大己貴命]]と[[少彦名命]]を祀ったことが起源だと云われている{{Cite web |title=神社由緒 |url=http://musashimitakejinja.jp/history/yuisho/ |website=武蔵御嶽神社【公式サイト】 |access-date=2024-07-12 |language=ja |publisher=武蔵御嶽神社}}。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の[[大臣命]]」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:阿倍氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 武渟川別命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武渟川別、武渟河別、建沼河別命、建沼河別、建沼河命、武沼河別命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 大夫 | 主君 = [[崇神天皇]]、[[垂仁天皇]] | 氏族 = [[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[大彦命]]『日本書紀』 | 兄弟 = [[御間城姫命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[豊韓別命]]『本朝皇胤紹運録』、大屋田子命 | 特記事項 = }} [[File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|thumb|220px|[[歌川国芳]]『日本百将伝』より武渟川別の想像図]] '''武渟川別'''（たけぬなかわわけ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「武渟川別」「武渟河別」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''建沼河別命'''（たけぬなかわわけのみこと）」と表記される。 第8代[[孝元天皇]][[皇子]]の[[大彦命]]の子で、[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣（阿倍氏）]]の祖。[[四道将軍]]の1人として[[東海道|東海]]に派遣されたほか、[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられる。 __TOC__{{-}} == 記録 ==
'''『[[日本書紀]]』に基づく関係系図''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[孝元天皇]]|02=[[吉備津彦命]]
［西道］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[開化天皇]]|02=[[大彦命]]
［北陸］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[彦坐王|○]]|02={{Supra|10}} [[崇神天皇]]|03=[[御間城姫|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}]]|04='''武渟川別'''
［東海］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01=[[丹波道主命]]
（丹波）|02={{Supra|11}} [[垂仁天皇]]}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の[[大臣命]]」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] * [[武蔵御嶽神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:阿倍氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no..."
adding original pages categories to all the pages I newly created today
adding new pages tag because that is one I use for finding new links

『[[日本書紀]]』崇神天皇10年9月9日条では武渟川別を[[東海道|東海]]に派遣するとあり、同書では[[北陸道|北陸]]に派遣された[[大彦命]]、[[山陽道|西道]]に派遣された[[吉備津彦命]]、[[丹波国|丹波]]に派遣された[[丹波道主命]]とともに「[[四道将軍]]」と総称されている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。その後、将軍らは崇神天皇10年10月22日に出発し、崇神天皇11年4月28日に平定を報告した。 また同書崇神天皇60年7月14日条によると、天皇の命により武渟川別は吉備津彦と共に[[出雲振根]]を誅殺している{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。垂仁天皇25年2月8日条では、[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）・[[大伴武日|武日]]（[[大伴氏|大伴連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、四道将軍としての4人の派遣ではないが、やはり崇神天皇の時に大毘古命（大彦命）は高志道に、建沼河別命は東方十二道に派遣されたとする。そして大毘古命と建沼河別命が出会った地が「相津」（現・[[福島県]][[会津]]）と名付けられた、と地名起源説話を伝える。 == 後裔氏族 == 武渟川別について、『日本書紀』では「阿倍臣遠祖」、『古事記』では「阿倍臣等の祖」として、いずれも[[阿倍氏]]の祖であるとしている。 また『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 竹田臣 - 阿倍朝臣同祖。大彦命男の武渟川別命の後{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 そのほか『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「[[国造本紀]]」では、[[那須国造]]（[[栃木県]]北東部を支配）について「建沼河命の孫の[[大臣命]]」に始まると記されているが、この「建沼河命」は武渟川別に比定される「那須国」『国史大辞典』 [[吉川弘文館]]。。 == 考証 == 武渟川別が派遣された「東方十二道」は会津に至るまでの12ヶ国を意味するが、これは後世の国制を反映したものと見られる{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}}。この武渟川別を含む四道将軍の史実性の評価には諸説がある。詳しくは「[[四道将軍]]」を参照。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[四道将軍]] * [[伊佐須美神社]] * [[武蔵御嶽神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たけぬなかわわけ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:阿倍氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''Genealogy Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[Emperor Kōrei|孝霊天皇]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen|孝元天皇]]|02=[[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|吉備津彦命]]
[Western Path]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[Emperor Kaika|開化天皇]]|02=[[Ōhiko no Mikoto|大彦命]]
[Northern Path]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|彦坐王]]|02={{Supra|10}} [[Emperor Sujin|崇神天皇]]|03=[[Himeimaki no Mikoto|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}]]|04='''Takenukawawake'''
[Eastern Path]}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takeninukawawake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takeninukawawake}}[[category:kk]] redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takeninukawawake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Ancestors of Japanese clans]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takeninukawawake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takeninukawawake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''Genealogy Based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[Emperor Kōrei|孝霊天皇}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen|孝元天皇]]|02=[[Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|吉備津彦命]]
[Western Path]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[Emperor Kaika|開化天皇]]|02=[[Ōhiko no Mikoto|大彦命]]
[Northern Path]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01=[[Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|彦坐王]]|02={{Supra|10}} [[Emperor Sujin|崇神天皇]]|03=[[Himeimaki no Mikoto|{{color|#FC4E6B|御間城姫}}|04='''Takenukawawake'''
[Eastern Path]}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite revision.text:
'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite revision.text:
'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] revision.text:
'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] （Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===

'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite revision.text:
'''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō region|ja|東海道}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Mimaki-hime}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko no Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|ja|会津}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko no Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013年|p=38-59}} == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013年}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}} * {{ill|Musashi Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]
