［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]|03=[[:en:Mimaki-hime|{{color]]}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day [[:en:Aizu|Aizu]] in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]] * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He was sent to the [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tōkaidō region]]. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]] * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.comment: Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
revision.text:
[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546050]] Wikidata name: Takenunakawawake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tōkaidō region]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko]]
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]|03=[[:en:Mimaki-hime|{{color]]}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day [[:en:Aizu|Aizu]] in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]] * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko]]
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]|03=[[:en:Mimaki-hime|{{color]]}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]] were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day [[:en:Aizu|Aizu]] in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]] * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546050]] Wikidata name: Takenunakawawake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the [[:en:Tōkaidō (region)|Tōkaidō region]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===

［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]|03=[[:en:Mimaki-hime|{{color]]}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''

The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]] * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546050]] Wikidata name: Takenunakawawake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō region}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|en|Mimaki-hime|WD=Q11071228|lt={{color}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11546050]] Wikidata name: Takenunakawawake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō region}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|en|Mimaki-hime|WD=Q11071228|lt={{color}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō region}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===

The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

revision.text:
[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228}}}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō region}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|en|Mimaki-hime|WD=Q11071228|lt={{color}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===
'''『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』-Based Genealogical Chart''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|7}} {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|8}} {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|02={{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}}
［Western Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||||)|-|-|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|||02| 01={{Supra|9}} {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}|02={{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
［Northern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|(|||||)|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|y|~|03|04| 01={{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|ja|彦坐王|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu|qq=}}|02={{Supra|10}} {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}|03={{ill|Mimaki-hime|en|Mimaki-hime|WD=Q11071228|lt={{color}}}}|04='''Take Nunakawawake'''
［Eastern Route］}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02| 01={{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu|1=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine|1=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] [[ja:武渟川別]][[zh:武渟川别]] {{Infobox person|image=File:武渟川別 Takenukawawake 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252|1=Yamato Kingship}}|children={{ill|Abe kogoto|WD=Q97613711|1=Abe kogoto}}; {{ill|Q109290210|WD=Q109290210|1=Obuhi no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Mimakihime|WD=Q11071228|1=Mimaki-hime}}}} '''Takenukawawake''' (武渟川別命, ''Takenukawawake''), also referred to as ''Takenunakawawake'' or ''Takenukawawake no Mikoto,'' is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' refers to him as "Takenukawawake" or "Takenukawabake," while the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' uses the name "Takenunakawawake no Mikoto" (建沼河別命). He is known as a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, and is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}. Takenukawawake was one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, who were dispatched to pacify different regions of Japan. He was sent to the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō region|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, he was recognized as one of the five ''maetsukimi'' (大夫), or "chief retainers." == Historical Records == === Genealogy ===

（Tanba Region）|02={{Supra|11}} {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree/end}}
The ''Nihon Shoki'' states that Takenukawawake was sent to the Tōkaidō region on September 9 of the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is mentioned alongside {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (dispatched to the Hokuriku region), {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} (sent to the Sanyō region), and {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}} (sent to Tanba Province). These four commanders were collectively called the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Takenukawawake and his fellow commanders began their expeditions on October 22 and successfully reported the pacification of their assigned regions on April 28 of the following year. In the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, Takenukawawake and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} were ordered to execute {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} for treason. During the reign of Emperor Suinin, he was one of five ''maetsukimi'' tasked with overseeing religious rituals and ensuring harmony between the imperial court and the deities.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} === ''Kojiki'' Account === The ''Kojiki'' similarly mentions Takenukawawake's involvement during Emperor Sujin's reign but describes his expedition differently. According to this account, he and Ōhiko-no-Mikoto met in a place called "Aitsu" (modern-day {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|WD=Q1205071|lt=Aizu|1=Aizu}} in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}), which was named in reference to their encounter. This tale is part of a series of {{ill|place-name origin stories|ja|地名起源説話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place-name origin stories|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Descendants == Takenukawawake is recognized as the ancestor of the Abe clan. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' specifically lists the following clans as his descendants: * '''Takeda no Atai''' (竹田臣) – Recognized as a descendant of Takenukawawake through Ōhiko-no-Mikoto.{{Sfn|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }} Additionally, the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' states that the {{ill|Nasu clan|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan|qq=}}, rulers of the northeastern part of {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, trace their origins to Takenukawawake.{{Sfn|国史大辞典}} == Historical Evaluation == Takenukawawake's mission to the "Eastern Twelve Regions" may reflect later administrative structures rather than historical reality. The historicity of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads remains a topic of debate.{{Sfn|宝賀|2013rd year |p=38-59}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=武渟川別|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|武渟川別（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|year=2013|chapter=|title=阿倍氏 四道将軍の後裔たち|publisher=[[青垣出版]]|isbn=978-4434176753|ref={{Harvid|宝賀|2013rd year }}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Figures Before the Asuka Period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} * {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine|1=Isasumi Shrine}} * {{ill|Musashi-Mitake Shrine|ja|武蔵御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Mitake Shrine|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|武渟川別|version=103240029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:武渟川別]] [[zh:武渟川别]] {{wikidata link|Q11546050}} [[Category:Abe clan]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
